minutes_briefs.title: BC updates electric vehicle definition and adds new 'EV OK' sign to Motor Vehicle Act regulations
minutes_briefs.summary: British Columbia has amended its Motor Vehicle Act Regulations to update the definition of an 'electric motor vehicle' and introduce a new 'electric vehicle OK' sign. The updated definition now includes vehicles powered solely by a hydrogen fuel cell, in addition to those with a battery that can be charged from an external source. The new 'EV OK' sign replaces the previous reference to a generic 'traffic control device' in section 42.02(5), giving drivers clearer guidance on where electric vehicles are permitted.
minutes_briefs.action_required: No immediate action required — but update your fleet and signage policies to reflect the new 'EV OK' sign and expanded electric vehicle definition.

key_dates.label: BC Motor Vehicle Act regulation amendment takes effect

minutes_briefs.source_body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 59/2026
Made under: Motor Vehicle Act
Published in: No. 7 – B.C. Regs. 57 to 61, April 21, 2026
Deposit date: April 20, 2026

B.C. Reg. 59/2026, deposited April 20, 2026, under theMOTOR VEHICLE ACT[section 209 (1) and (2)]. Order in Council 152/2026, approved and ordered April 20, 2026. On the recommendation of the undersigned, the Lieutenant Governor, by and with the advice and consent of the Executive Council, orders that the Motor Vehicle Act Regulations, B.C. Reg. 26/58, are amended as set out in the attached Schedule. — M. FARNWORTH,Minister of Transportation and Transit; L. POPHAM,Presiding Member of the Executive Council. 1	Schedule 1 of Division 23 of the Motor Vehicle Act Regulations, B.C. Reg. 26/58, is amended by adding the following sign before the "TRUCK SIGNS (Regulatory)" signs: (a)	by repealing the definition of"electric motor vehicle"and substituting the following: "electric motor vehicle"means one of the following: (i)	an electric propulsion engine, and (ii)	a battery storage system that can be charged from an external electric charging system; (b)	a motor vehicle that is propelled solely by an electric motor powered by a hydrogen fuel cell;, and (b)	by adding the following definition: "electric vehicle OK sign"means the "ELECTRIC VEHICLE OK" sign depicted and described in Schedule 1 of Division 23;. 3	Section 42.02 (5) is amended by striking out "a traffic control device" and substituting "an electric vehicle OK sign".
